rows.source: Source-parcel inventory (3 parcels, 324,542 ha total, western Amazonas; identities held privately)
rows.note: Standing-forest area of the three Amazonas source parcels (the asset).

rows.source: GLEAM4 (Sci Data 2025); HESS 25:2279 (2021) Amazon ET ~3-4 mm/day
rows.note: Mean evapotranspiration of intact Amazon forest.

rows.source: RECON moisture-flow cube (De Petrillo, Monaco et al., Politecnico di Torino; Zenodo 14191920; UTrack-based, ERA5-reconciled via Iterative Proportional Fitting, 2008-2017)
rows.note: REAL (Phase 1, Gate 1B precomputed). Fraction of source-box ET that precipitates over the soy/La Plata sink, from the RECON source->sink flow cube integrated over the source box (lat -8..-4, lon -72.5..-62) -> sink box (lat -34..-10, lon -64..-45). value = ET-weighted box mean; sd = spatial spread across the 198 source cells (the box-vs-parcel proxy uncertainty). Validation: source-box outflow matches ERA5 ET to ratio 1.000; Gate-1 4a Amazon->La Plata = 21.5% (within Zemp et al. 2014 [12,35]%). CROSS-VALIDATED (Phase 1B) by the AMSSRAB 3-model ensemble (WAM2layers Eulerian + UTrack + WaterSip Lagrangian; Zenodo 17032120): all three independent models put Amazon->La Plata at 16-20% (growing season), inside Zemp, corroborating this benchmark; DJF structural spread w = 0.33 +/- 0.05. Hence confidence='medium-high'. Value/sd stay on the RECON annual basis; the full annual ensemble (all 4 seasons) is the remaining, bandwidth-limited step. See data/moisture/gate1b_report.md and src/fluvion/data/load_amssrab.py. w is INTENSIVE; A_SOURCE_HA stays the parcel/asset area, NOT the box (see A_SOURCE_HA note and src/fluvion/geo.py). Supersedes the 0.060 synthetic placeholder.

rows.source: IBGE / CONAB soy area, Southern Brazil + Mato Grosso (illustrative extent)
rows.note: Downwind sink area over which Delta-rainfall is spread.

rows.source: CHIRPS annual rainfall, soy belt (~1800 mm/yr; cf. Leite-Filho et al. 2024 IJC baseline)
rows.note: Annual baseline rainfall for the elasticity denominator. The published elasticity is calibrated on ANNUAL rainfall, so dR and this baseline are both annual (mm/yr) and the ratio is basis-consistent. A growing-season-resolved baseline (with monthly footprints) is a Phase-1 upgrade; do NOT approximate it with a months/12 scalar, which double-counts the seasonal mechanism already embedded in the empirical elasticity.

rows.source: Scenario axis: Flores et al. Nature 2024 (10-47% exposed by 2050)
rows.note: Scenario: share of the source's moisture-recycling service lost under the tipping case. NOT backtestable (droughts are ENSO/transport anomalies, not deforestation counterfactuals) -> confidence='low' per the project rule; reported as a scenario RANGE, not a point. Flores anchors exposure risk, not a direct moisture-loss coefficient. Scenario centers (0.25/0.50/0.75) are the moisture_service_loss levels in config/corridor_amazonas_soybelt.yaml.

rows.source: Batista, Duku & Hein 2023 Ecol Modelling (S0304380023002636: 6.6% soy yield gap from deforestation-driven rainfall change); Leite-Filho et al. 2024 Int J Climatology (10.1002/joc.8381: ~360 mm annual rainfall decline); ~1800 mm/yr CHIRPS baseline (assumption)
rows.note: Elasticity of soy yield to ANNUAL rainfall. Derived: 6.6% yield gap / (360 mm / 1800 mm) = 6.6% / 20% = 0.33, every term on an annual basis (so no separate season scaling is applied downstream). Corroborated in magnitude by Flach/Leite-Filho PNAS 2025 (10.1073/pnas.2525378123: 6-30% seasonal precip decline, ~6% Rio Grande do Sul loss) and by the rainfall-vs-deforestation mechanism of Leite-Filho et al. Nat Commun 2021 (10.1038/s41467-021-22840-7) -- neither of which reports this elasticity directly. Linear approximation; the underlying response is piecewise-linear with thresholds (MARS), so this overestimates loss at extreme rainfall deficits.

rows.source: CONAB / IBGE soy production, exposed municipalities (illustrative)
rows.note: Baseline soy production exposed to the cascade.

rows.source: CEPEA / CBOT soybean price series (GBP 350/t at GBP->USD 1.3186, 26 Jun 2026)
Trase / UN Comtrade / FAOSTAT exposure placeholder for the UK-relevant South American soy book in the Amazon-influenced precipitation system
rows.note: PLACEHOLDER. Share of affected production exposed to the defined UK book. Used for the RISK number only (UK-attributable VaR); per-state shares pending.
Discount rate for the avoided-loss annuity. Sampled uniform 4-12% (value 0.08 +/- sd 0.04) to capture sensitivity to this key valuation assumption, spanning a green ~4% to a commercial ~12% rate.

rows.source: Assumption; matches dieback NPV horizon (Global Tipping Points 2023)

rows.note: Amazon->La Plata 21.5% within Zemp [12,35]%; AMSSRAB 3-model cross-check (16-21%).

rows.source: INCRA Atlas do Mercado de Terras 2025 (ref. Dec 2024), AM-1502
rows.note: All-use VTI R$1,525.62/ha x FX BRL->USD 0.1934; degraded-pasture VTN ~$210 lower anchor; cross-check Receita Federal VTN 2022.

rows.quantity: Baker et al. 2026 rainfall-generation value (annual-flow cross-check)
rows.source: Baker et al. 2026, Quantifying tropical forest rainfall generation, Comms Earth & Environment 7:150, doi:10.1038/s43247-025-03159-3
rows.note: Published US$59.40/ha/yr (Amazon-forest rainfall generation); US$20 +/- 7 bn/yr Brazilian Legal Amazon. Per-ha sd inherited from the aggregate band (35%). Engine annual-equiv ~$31/ha/yr is ~half. Compare to annual-flow, NOT the 30-yr NPV.
Sign/rank skill at state resolution; cluster-robust p~0.004.

_note: Every reported quantity with source + confidence. The audit trail.
